English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Marjorie Knoll papers
Knoll, Marjorie.
6787
.4 cubic feet. .4 cubic feet.
1954-1999 Correspondence, Publications, Photographs. Correspondence, lectures, publications, awards, and photographs, documenting Knoll's involvement in AAFCS and her career as a home economist at Penn State University. Collection material in English 6198860
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2008
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, February 2008
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Marjorie Knoll received her Bachelor of Science degree (1942) and Masters of Arts degree (1947) from the University of Nebraska and her Doctor of Philosophy degree (1954) from Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, lectures, publications, awards, and photographs, documenting Knoll's involvement in AAFCS and her career as a home economist at Penn State University.
Cite As:
Marjorie Knoll papers, #6787. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Home economics -- Study and teaching Pennsylvania State University. Department of Household Economics and Management American Association of Family and Consumer Sciences
